# 3 debates

*Original link:* http://yamlb.wordpress.com/2006/08/06/3-debates/  
*pubDate:* Sun, 06 Aug 2006 19:55:29 +0000  
*post_id:* 43  
*status:* publish  
*type:* post

**Content (verbatim)**

The following question can either start a discussion on machine learning or on Philosophy:

Is Bayes enough ?
Is Turing enough ?
Is Learning enough ?

**Comments**

---
comment_id: 49
author: aslan
date: 2006-08-06 20:25:23
approved: 1

computer science considers Turing to be sufficient for computation...  how can bayesian inference be enough? 

---
comment_id: 50
author: Pierre
date: 2006-08-07 08:55:16
approved: 1

Not sure I understand you.
Maybe you are answering: one of your question should be answered by yes and the others by no.

The turing machine is a model for computing, so yes turing is enough for computing.

Bayes is a model for inference (maybe the best one) (including non-determinism) and learning is an instance of inference, so yes.

Learning is an instance of inference, so how could it be enough ?

I may be wrong...

---
comment_id: 51
author: Pierre
date: 2006-08-07 08:58:59
approved: 1

And about Bayes being enough. The prob. calculus is not enough to represent ignorance, paradoxes, fuzzy facts, inconsistent theories, first order logic.... Maybe ambiguity and information functions will propose some compromise between under specififed representation and inference tractability...
